revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] to [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE [[:en:Japanese dictionary|Japanese dictionary]] of [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]]. The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name=OxfordRef_Ekisai>{{Cite web |title=Kariya Ekisai |url=https://www.oxfordreference.com/view/10.1093/oi/authority.20110803100030407 |access-date=2021-03-23 |website=Oxford Reference }}</ref>
<ref name=MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori>For more details, see Bailey, Don Clifford. (1960). "Early Japanese Lexicography". ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 16:1-52. pp. 4–6, 18–9. in English, and Mori Shiten 林史典. (1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. Tokyo: Ōfū. {{ISBN|4-273-02890-5}} in Japanese.</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{cite book |last=Minamoto |first=Shitagō |author-link=Minamoto no Shitagō |date=1968 |orig-date=c. 934 |title=Shohon Shūsei Wamyō Ruijushō: Honbunhen |location=Kyōto |publisher=Rinsen |isbn=4-653-00507-9 }} * {{cite book |last=Minamoto |first=Shitagō |author-link=Minamoto no Shitagō |date=1968 |title=Shohon Shūsei Wamyō Ruijushō: Sakuinhen |location=Kyōto |publisher=Rinsen |isbn=4-653-00509-5 }} * {{cite book |last=Minamoto |first=Shitagō |author-link=Minamoto no Shitagō |date=1966 |orig-date=c. 934 |title=Shohon Shūsei Wamyō Ruijushō: Gaihen |location=Kyōto |publisher=Rinsen |isbn=4-653-00508-7 }}
==External links== *[http://www.littera.waseda.ac.jp/wamyou/ 倭名類聚鈔] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20061112061038/http://www.littera.waseda.ac.jp/wamyou/ |date=2006-11-12 }}, Waseda University Library online ''Wamyō ruijushō'' (in Japanese) *Manuscript scans at [[:en:Waseda University Library|Waseda University Library]]: [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_06479/ 1617], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_06263/ 1667], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_06837/ 1667], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_00180/ 1688], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_04312/ 17th century], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_05342/ 17th century]
{{Dictionaries of Japanese}} ]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE [[:en:Japanese dictionary|Japanese dictionary]] of [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]]. The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name=OxfordRef_Ekisai>{{Cite web |title=Kariya Ekisai |url=https://www.oxfordreference.com/view/10.1093/oi/authority.20110803100030407 |access-date=2021-03-23 |website=Oxford Reference }}</ref>
<ref name=MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori>For more details, see Bailey, Don Clifford. (1960). "Early Japanese Lexicography". ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 16:1-52. pp. 4–6, 18–9. in English, and Mori Shiten 林史典. (1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. Tokyo: Ōfū. {{ISBN|4-273-02890-5}} in Japanese.</ref> }}
==Further reading== * {{cite book |last=Minamoto |first=Shitagō |author-link=Minamoto no Shitagō |date=1968 |orig-date=c. 934 |title=Shohon Shūsei Wamyō Ruijushō: Honbunhen |location=Kyōto |publisher=Rinsen |isbn=4-653-00507-9 }} * {{cite book |last=Minamoto |first=Shitagō |author-link=Minamoto no Shitagō |date=1968 |title=Shohon Shūsei Wamyō Ruijushō: Sakuinhen |location=Kyōto |publisher=Rinsen |isbn=4-653-00509-5 }} * {{cite book |last=Minamoto |first=Shitagō |author-link=Minamoto no Shitagō |date=1966 |orig-date=c. 934 |title=Shohon Shūsei Wamyō Ruijushō: Gaihen |location=Kyōto |publisher=Rinsen |isbn=4-653-00508-7 }}
==External links== *[http://www.littera.waseda.ac.jp/wamyou/ 倭名類聚鈔] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20061112061038/http://www.littera.waseda.ac.jp/wamyou/ |date=2006-11-12 }}, Waseda University Library online ''Wamyō ruijushō'' (in Japanese) *Manuscript scans at [[:en:Waseda University Library|Waseda University Library]]: [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_06479/ 1617], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_06263/ 1667], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_06837/ 1667], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_00180/ 1688], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_04312/ 17th century], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_05342/ 17th century]
{{Dictionaries of Japanese}} ]]

The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name=OxfordRef_Ekisai>{{Cite web |title=Kariya Ekisai |url=https://www.oxfordreference.com/view/10.1093/oi/authority.20110803100030407 |access-date=2021-03-23 |website=Oxford Reference }}</ref>
<ref name=MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori>For more details, see Bailey, Don Clifford. (1960). "Early Japanese Lexicography". ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 16:1-52. pp. 4–6, 18–9. in English, and Mori Shiten 林史典. (1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. Tokyo: Ōfū. {{ISBN|4-273-02890-5}} in Japanese.</ref> }}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE [[:en:Japanese dictionary|Japanese dictionary]] of [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]]. The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

revision.comment: Add page with conflicting Wikidata QIDs
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE [[:en:Japanese dictionary|Japanese dictionary]] of [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]]. The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

(1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. Tokyo: Ōfū. {{ISBN|4-273-02890-5}} in Japanese.</ref> }}

In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE [[:en:Japanese dictionary|Japanese dictionary]] of [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]]. The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

(1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. Tokyo: Ōfū. {{ISBN|4-273-02890-5}} in Japanese.</ref> }}

In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE [[:en:Japanese dictionary|Japanese dictionary]] of [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]]. The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

(1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. Tokyo: Ōfū. {{ISBN|4-273-02890-5}} in Japanese.</ref> }}

In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[commons:" to "[[co:commons:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE [[:en:Japanese dictionary|Japanese dictionary]] of [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]]. The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

(1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. Tokyo: Ōfū. {{ISBN|4-273-02890-5}} in Japanese.</ref> }}

A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: categorize pages with unclosed brackets
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE [[:en:Japanese dictionary|Japanese dictionary]] of [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]]. The [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] scholar [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]] (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]]'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language [[:en:thesaurus|thesaurus]]. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] [[:en:Collation|Collation]] system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''[[:en:Erya|Erya]]'', ''[[:en:Xiao Erya|Xiao Erya]]'', and ''[[:en:Shiming|Shiming]]''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''[[:en:Kanji|Kanji]]'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a [[:en:homonym|homonym]] or ''[[:en:fanqie|fanqie]]'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''[[:en:Man'yōgana|Man'yōgana]]'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''[[:en:Shuowen Jiezi|Shuowen Jiezi]]'') and Japanese (the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! [[:en:Kanji|Kanji]] ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for [[:en:Japanese encyclopedias|Japanese encyclopedias]]. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>

(1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Wamyō ruijushō]][[de:Wamyō ruijushō]][[en:Wamyō Ruijushō]][[fa:وامیو رویجوشو]][[fr:Wamyō ruijushō]][[it:Wamyō ruijushō]][[ja:和名類聚抄]][[ko:화명류취초]][[ru:Вамё Руйдзюсё]][[uk:Вамьо Руйдзюсьо]][[zh:和名類聚抄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2702542]] Wikidata name: Wamyō Ruijushō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese dictionary of Chinese characters}} {{ref-improve|date=February 2016}} {{italic title}} [[File:Вамё Руйдзюсё.jpg|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''''Wamyō ruijushō''''' or '''''Wamyō ruijūshō'''''|和名類聚抄||"Japanese names [for things], classified and annotated"}} is a 938 CE {{ill|Japanese dictionary|en|Japanese dictionary|WD=Q6158568|lt=Japanese dictionary}} of {{ill|Chinese characters|en|Chinese characters|WD=Q8201|lt=Chinese characters}}. The {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} scholar {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|en|Minamoto no Shitagō|WD=Q1559382|lt=Minamoto no Shitagō}} (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo}}'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language {{ill|thesaurus|en|thesaurus|WD=Q179797|lt=thesaurus}}. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] {{ill|Collation|en|Collation|WD=Q1601543|lt=Collation}} system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''{{ill|Erya|en|Erya|WD=Q1207685|lt=Erya}}'', ''{{ill|Xiao Erya|en|Xiao Erya|WD=Q8044209|lt=Xiao Erya}}'', and ''{{ill|Shiming|en|Shiming|WD=Q5927580|lt=Shiming}}''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji}}'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a {{ill|homonym|en|homonym|WD=Q160843|lt=homonym}} or ''{{ill|fanqie|en|fanqie|WD=Q1899324|lt=fanqie}}'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''{{ill|Man'yōgana|en|Man'yōgana|WD=Q862803|lt=Man'yōgana}}'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''{{ill|Shuowen Jiezi|en|Shuowen Jiezi|WD=Q1072348|lt=Shuowen Jiezi}}'') and Japanese (the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). The 10-volume edition has 24 main headings divided into a total of 128 subheadings, while the 20-volume version has 32 and 249, respectively. The table below illustrates how words are semantically categorized in the 10-volume edition.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Subject headings in the 1883 ''Wamyō ruijushō''
! ! [[:en:Rōmaji|Rōmaji]] ! {{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji}} ! Translation ! Subjects |- | 1 | ''Tenchi'' | 天地 | Universe | constellations, weather, gods, earth, topography |- | 2 | ''Jinrin'' | 人倫 |Humans | gender, kinship, family, marriage |- | 3 | ''Keitai'' | 形体 | Body | body parts, sense organs, internal organs |- | 4 | ''Shippei'' | 疾病 | Sickness | diseases, wounds |- | 5 | ''Jutsugei'' | 術藝 | Arts | martial arts, fine arts, skills |- | 6 | ''Kyosho'' | 居處 | Architecture | houses, walls, doors, roads |- | 7 | ''Sensha'' | 舟車 | Vehicles | boats, carts, carriages |- | 8 | ''Chinpō'' | 珍寶 | Treasures | precious metals, jewels |- | 9 | ''Fuhaku'' | 布帛 | Textiles | embroidery, silks, woven fabrics |- | 10 | ''Shōzoku'' | 装束 | Clothing | hats, clothes, belts, shoes |- | 11 | ''Inshoku'' | 飲食 | Foods and Drinks | liquors, beverages, cooked grains, fruits, meats |- | 12 | ''Kibei'' | 器皿 | Utensils | objects of metal, lacquer, wood, tile, and bamboo |- | 13 | ''Tōka'' | 燈火 | Illumination | lamps, lights, lighting |- | 14 | ''Chōdo'' | 調度 | Things and Supplies | implements, tools, weapons, utensils, furnishings |- | 15 | ''Uzoku'' | 羽族 | Birds | birds, feathers, ornithology |- | 16 | ''Mōgun'' | 毛群 | Wild Animals | wild animals, body parts |- | 17 | ''Gyūba'' | 牛馬 | Domestic Animals | cattle, horses, sheep, body parts, diseases |- | 18 | ''Ryōgo'' | 龍魚 | Aquatic animals | dragons, fish, reptiles, amphibians |- | 19 | ''Kibai'' | 龜貝 | Shellfishes | turtles, shellfish |- | 20 | ''Chūchi'' | 蟲豸 | Miscellaneous Animals | insects, worms, small reptiles |- | 21 | ''Tōkoku'' | 稲穀 | Grains | rices, cereals |- | 22 | ''Saiso'' | 菜蔬 | Vegetables | tubers, seaweeds, edible plants |- | 23 | ''Kayu'' | 果蓏 | Fruits | fruits, melons |- | 24 | ''Sōmoku'' | 草木 | Plants | grasses, mosses, vines, flowers, trees |}
The broadly inclusive ''Wamyō ruijushō'' dictionary was an antecedent for {{ill|Japanese encyclopedias|en|Japanese encyclopedias|WD=Q2797676|lt=Japanese encyclopedias}}. In the present day, it provides linguists and historians with an invaluable record of the Japanese language over a thousand years ago,{{r|MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori}} more specifically the linguistics of the 10th century and Heian Period.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wamyō Ruijushō • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/wamyo-ruijusho/|access-date=2021-12-06|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref>
==References== {{Reflist|refs= <ref name=OxfordRef_Ekisai>{{Cite web |title=Kariya Ekisai |url=https://www.oxfordreference.com/view/10.1093/oi/authority.20110803100030407 |access-date=2021-03-23 |website=Oxford Reference }}</ref>
<ref name=MoreDetails_BaileyOkimori>For more details, see Bailey, Don Clifford. (1960). "Early Japanese Lexicography". ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 16:1-52. pp. 4–6, 18–9. in English, and Mori Shiten 林史典. (1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288. Tokyo: Ōfū. {{ISBN|4-273-02890-5}} in Japanese.</ref> }}

The {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} scholar {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|en|Minamoto no Shitagō|WD=Q1559382|lt=Minamoto no Shitagō}} (源順, 911–983 CE) began compilation in 934, at the request of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo}}'s daughter. This ''Wamyō ruijushō'' title is abbreviated as ''Wamyōshō'', and has graphic variants of 和名類聚抄 with ''wa'' [[Wikt:和|和]] "harmony; Japan" for ''[[:en:Wa (Japan)|wa]]'' [[Wikt:倭|倭]] "dwarf; Japan" and 倭名類聚鈔 with ''shō'' [[Wikt:鈔|鈔]] "copy; summarize" for ''shō'' [[Wikt:抄|抄]] "copy; annotate".
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'' is the oldest extant Japanese dictionary organized into semantic headings, analogous to a Western language {{ill|thesaurus|en|thesaurus|WD=Q179797|lt=thesaurus}}. This ancient [[:en:lexicographical|lexicographical]] {{ill|Collation|en|Collation|WD=Q1601543|lt=Collation}} system was developed in [[:en:Chinese dictionaries|Chinese dictionaries]] like the ''{{ill|Erya|en|Erya|WD=Q1207685|lt=Erya}}'', ''{{ill|Xiao Erya|en|Xiao Erya|WD=Q8044209|lt=Xiao Erya}}'', and ''{{ill|Shiming|en|Shiming|WD=Q5927580|lt=Shiming}}''. The ''Wamyōshō'' categorizes ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|WD=Q82772|lt=Kanji}}'' vocabulary, primarily [[:en:nouns|nouns]], into main headings (''bu'' [[Wikt:部|部]]) divided into subheadings (''rui'' [[Wikt:類|類]]). For instance, the ''tenchi'' (天地 "heaven and earth") heading includes eight semantic divisions like ''seishuku'' (星宿 "stars and constellations"), ''un'u'' (雲雨 "clouds and rain"), and ''fūsetsu'' (風雪 "wind and snow").
Each dictionary entry gives the Chinese character, sources cited, [[:en:Chinese pronunciation|Chinese pronunciation]]s (with either a {{ill|homonym|en|homonym|WD=Q160843|lt=homonym}} or ''{{ill|fanqie|en|fanqie|WD=Q1899324|lt=fanqie}}'' spelling), definitions, and corresponding Japanese readings (in the ancient ''{{ill|Man'yōgana|en|Man'yōgana|WD=Q862803|lt=Man'yōgana}}'' system using kanji to represent Japanese pronunciation). It cites over 290 sources, both Chinese (for example, the ''{{ill|Shuowen Jiezi|en|Shuowen Jiezi|WD=Q1072348|lt=Shuowen Jiezi}}'') and Japanese (the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'').
The ''Wamyō ruijushō'', survives in both a 10-volume edition (十巻本) and a 20-volume edition (二十巻本). The larger one was published in 1617 with a commentary by [[Nawa Dōen]] (那波道円, 1595–1648) and was used in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} until the 1883 publication of the 10-volume edition annotated by [[Kariya Ekisai]] (狩谷棭齋, 1775–1835),{{r|OxfordRef_Ekisai}} also known as the ''Senchū Wamyō ruijushō'' (箋注倭名類聚抄 "Annotated commentary to the ''Wamyō ruijushō''"). (1996). "和名類聚抄 (''Wamyō ruijushō'')." In ''Nihon jisho jiten'' 日本辞書辞典 (''The Encyclopedia of Dictionaries Published in Japan''), Okimori Takuya 沖森卓也, et al., eds., pp. 287–288.
